Title: Suppression of Arabidopsis GGLT1 affects growth by reducing the L-galactose content and borate cross-linking of rhamnogalacturonan-II

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Golgi nucleotide-sugar transporter; GDP-sugar transporter; GONST3/GGLT1 family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; measured in rosette leaves in RNAi lines
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi hairpin construct in pHELLSGATE12; endogenous promoter for native gene expression; specific promoter not stated
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Rhamnogalacturonan-II (RG-II) L-galactose decoration and borate-cross-linked RG-II dimer
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains proper cell wall biochemical/biomechanical properties, plant growth and development, and cell wall integrity under salt stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces RG-II L-galactose and borate cross-linking when suppressed, causing impaired growth and cell wall integrity; increases ascorbate
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GGLT1 encodes a Golgi-localized GDP-sugar transporter required for normal RG-II structure. 2. RNAi suppression reduced terminal L-galactose on RG-II side-chain A by about 30–50% and reduced RG-II dimer abundance. 3. Reduced RG-II cross-linking caused growth defects that were rescued by borate supplementation and caused salt-sensitive cell wall integrity defects. 4. Loss of Golgi GDP-L-galactose utilization was associated with increased leaf ascorbic acid content.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; qPCR; fluorescent protein subcellular localization; RG-II structural analysis by SEC and MALDI-TOF MS; alpha-L-galactosidase digestion; cell wall monosaccharide analysis; borate supplementation rescue; salt stress root cell bursting assay; ascorbate quantification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-borate versus high-borate growth conditions; boric acid supplementation; salt stress (140 mM NaCl)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics/sphingolipidomics (LC-MS; Data S2); public gene expression data referenced; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to manipulate cell wall architecture, boron-use efficiency, biomass saccharification, and stress-related wall integrity; however strong suppression causes severe growth defects, so deployment would require partial/tissue-specific modulation rather than knockout
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong suppression likely lethal or non-viable; knockdown causes dwarf growth, altered wall composition under boron deficiency, and salt-sensitive cell bursting
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes GGLT1 is probably the primary Golgi GDP-L-galactose transporter for RG-II biosynthesis; not shown to affect xyloglucan fucosylation or GIPC glycosylation, helping distinguish it from related transporters GFT1 and GONST1
